header.description: CEO handles multi-lead, multi-plane, or multi-phase orchestration scenarios requiring dependency mapping, sequencing, and synthesis across leads

multi_lead_dispatch_required.description: Request requires dispatching to multiple leads simultaneously or sequentially — user request is horizontal, lead dispatches are vertical (one lane each) (per ceo.md lines 159-160, 340-345)
multi_lead_dispatch_required.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO dispatched to multiple leads

cross_lead_dependency_exists.description: One lead's output is required input to another lead's task — sequential dispatch is mandatory (per ceo.md lines 324-326, 449-450, Task Continuity)
cross_lead_dependency_exists.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO tracked cross-lead dependency and dispatched sequentially for dependent leads

result_synthesis_required.description: Multiple lead results must be synthesized into single coherent response — not raw concatenation or pass-through (per ceo.md lines 128, 328, 530)
result_synthesis_required.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO synthesis step combining multiple lead results into cohesive response

maps_lead_dependencies.description: CEO identifies all lead dependencies and maps dispatch order before sending any lead dispatch — parallel for independent leads, sequential for dependent leads (per ceo.md lines 324-326, 355-360, 449-450)
maps_lead_dependencies.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO mapped lead dependency graph before first multi-lead dispatch

synthesizes_multi_lead_results.description: CEO synthesizes multiple lead results into coherent structured response — preserves each lead's findings under instance labels, no silent merging (per ceo.md lines 328, 371-373, Aggregation Across Lead Instances)
synthesizes_multi_lead_results.observable_from_log: Execution log shows synthesis step combining lead results; each instance's return stands on its own under its label

escalates_with_complete_multi_lead_conte.description: CEO escalation includes status of all leads involved, not just the failing one — full multi-lead context package (per ceo.md lines 61-62, 492-495)
escalates_with_complete_multi_lead_conte.observable_from_log: Execution log shows escalation package includes complete multi-lead status: all lead results, rejection reasons, resolution attempts, specific blocker

does_not_dispatch_to_multiple_leads_unne.description: CEO does not dispatch to multiple leads when a single lead can handle the request — smallest pipeline that serves wins (per ceo.md lines 325, 539, Decision Heuristics)
does_not_dispatch_to_multiple_leads_unne.observable_from_log: YES if single-lead dispatch attempted first when sufficient; NO if multi-lead dispatch found when single lead could serve

does_not_begin_routing_until_full_scope_.description: CEO does not dispatch to any lead until the entire request scope is confirmed — horizontal decomposition must be complete before first dispatch (per ceo.md lines 159-160, 355-360)
does_not_begin_routing_until_full_scope_.observable_from_log: YES if CEO confirmed full scope before first dispatch; NO if dispatch found before full scope visible

does_not_synthesize_conflicting_results_.description: CEO does not silently resolve conflicts between lead results — surfaces conflicts to user for resolution (per ceo.md lines 138-139, 553-555, When Conflicts Appear)
does_not_synthesize_conflicting_results_.observable_from_log: YES if conflicts between lead results explicitly surfaced in CEO response; NO if conflicts silently resolved or hidden
